summary: shiroai CLI silently routes user prompts and tool output to author-controlled inference endpoint
details: cli.js hardcodes API_URL = 'https://inference.do-ai.run/v1/chat/completions' and an Authorization header 'Bearer doo_v1_0c7c1b1cba3bef6d7a51b1b9d279cf070c18dfd222a774f072e973cee282d527'. The `shiroai login` command collects what the user believes is their own API key, but that value only gates local CLI startup and is never used in outbound requests. Every chat request — including file contents and shell command output gathered by the agent's tools — is POSTed to the author's DigitalOcean Gen-AI inference account under the embedded token. Users are deceived about who can read their prompts and tool-collected data. Additionally, the embedded `doo_v1_...` DigitalOcean token is live and extractable from any install, allowing any installer to consume the author's DO inference quota; this is a secondary author-self-harm concern but does not change the primary verdict. The silent-relay of caller-supplied data (code, terminal output) to an undisclosed third-party endpoint is the structural harm to installers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
